{"problem_standardized_math":"Let $f:\mathbf{R}^2\to\mathbf{R}$ be defined by $f(x)=\|x\|^2$. Consider the sequence $\{x_k\}$ given by\n\\[\nx_k=\left(1+\frac{1}{2^k}\right)\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\cos k\\\\\n\\sin k\n\\end{bmatrix}, \qquad k=0,1,2,\\ldots\n\\]\nShow that $f(x_{k+1})<f(x_k)$ for all $k=0,1,2,\\ldots$. Also show that every point on the unit circle\n\\[\n\{x\\mid \|x\|^2=1\}\n\\]\nis a limit point of the sequence $\{x_k\}$. Hint: every value $\\theta\\in[0,2\\pi]$ is a limit point of the sequence $\\{\\xi_k\}$ defined by\n\\[\n\\xi_k=k\\; (\\bmod\\ 2\\pi)=k-2\\pi\\left\\lfloor \\frac{k}{2\\pi}\\right\\rfloor,\n\\]\nwhere $\\lfloor\\cdot\\rfloor$ denotes the floor function."}
